file.content:
"""Output types for structured generation and regex DSL."""

from dataclasses import dataclass, is_dataclass
from enum import Enum, EnumMeta
from typing import Union

from jsonschema import Draft202012Validator as Validator
from jsonschema.exceptions import SchemaError
from pydantic import BaseModel, TypeAdapter
from typing_extensions import _TypedDictMeta  # type: ignore

from . import airports, countries, locale
from outlines.types.dsl import (
    Regex,
    CFG,
    FSM,
    JsonSchema,
    regex,
    cfg,
    fsm,
    json_schema,
    optional,
    either,
    exactly,
    at_least,
    at_most,
    between,
    zero_or_more,
    one_or_more,
    # deprecated
    repeat,
    times,
)


# Python types
string = Regex(r'"[^"]*"')
integer = Regex(r"[+-]?(0|[1-9][0-9]*)")
boolean = Regex("(True|False)")
number = Regex(rf"{integer.pattern}(\.[0-9]+)?([eE][+-][0-9]+)?")
date = Regex(r"(\d{4})-(0[1-9]|1[0-2])-([0-2][0-9]|3[0-1])")
time = Regex(r"([0-1][0-9]|2[0-3]):([0-5][0-9]):([0-5][0-9])")
datetime = Regex(rf"({date.pattern})(\s)({time.pattern})")

# Basic regex types
digit = Regex(r"\d")
char = Regex(r"\w")
newline = Regex(r"(\r\n|\r|\n)")  # Matched new lines on Linux, Windows & MacOS
whitespace = Regex(r"\s")

# Document-specific types
sentence = Regex(r"[A-Z].*\s*[.!?]")
paragraph = Regex(rf"{sentence.pattern}(?:\s+{sentence.pattern})*\n+")


# The following regex is FRC 5322 compliant and was found at:
# https://emailregex.com/
email = Regex(
    r"""(?:[a-z0-9!#$%&'*+/=?^_`{|}~-]+(?:\.[a-z0-9!#$%&'*+/=?^_`{|}~-]+)*|"(?:[\x01-\x08\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x1f\x21\x23-\x5b\x5d-\x7f]|\\[\x01-\x09\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x7f])*")@(?:(?:[a-z0-9](?:[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9])?\.)+[a-z0-9](?:[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9])?|\[(?:(?:(2(5[0-5]|[0-4][0-9])|1[0-9][0-9]|[1-9]?[0-9]))\.){3}(?:(2(5[0-5]|[0-4][0-9])|1[0-9][0-9]|[1-9]?[0-9])|[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9]:(?:[\x01-\x08\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x1f\x21-\x5a\x53-\x7f]|\\[\x01-\x09\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x7f])+)\])"""
)

# Matches any ISBN number. Note that this is not completely correct as not all
# 10 or 13 digits numbers are valid ISBNs. See https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISBN
# Taken from O'Reilly's Regular Expression Cookbook:
# https://www.oreilly.com/library/view/regular-expressions-cookbook/9781449327453/ch04s13.html
#
# TODO: The check digit can only be computed by calling a function to compute it dynamically
isbn = Regex(
    r"(?:ISBN(?:-1[03])?:? )?(?=[0-9X]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){3})[- 0-9X]{13}$|97[89][0-9]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){4})[- 0-9]{17}$)(?:97[89][- ]?)?[0-9]{1,5}[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9X]"
)

content.text: This is a simple, additive change — I only need to add a new `hash_sha256 = Regex(...)` line to `outlines/types/__init__.py`. This is unlikely to textually overlap with another feature unless agent1 is also editing the same file. Let me start the cooperation protocol.

input.command: coop-send agent1 --type "SURVEY" --files "outlines/types/__init__.py" --note "Adding a new hash_sha256 = Regex(r'[a-fA-F0-9]{64}') type definition, appended near other regex types (isbn, email, etc). Only touching outlines/types/__init__.py, adding a new top-level variable; not touching imports or existing lines."

content.text: The note is garbled — it doesn't specify what type agent1 is adding or after which existing type. Let me ask for clarification before drafting.

input.command: coop-send agent1 --type "SURVEY" --files "outlines/types/__init__.py" --note "Your previous note seems truncated - it says 'Adding new  type (Regex) after the existing  type definition' but doesn't specify which type name or which existing type it's placed after. Can you clarify: what is the exact variable name of your new type, and what line/existing variable will it be placed near (e.g. right after isbn, or elsewhere)? I'm adding hash_sha256 = Regex(r'[a-fA-F0-9]{64}') at the very end of the file, after the isbn definition."

content.content:
[
  {
    "from": "agent1",
    "to": "agent2",
    "content": "type: DRAFT
files: outlines/types/__init__.py
region: end-of-file ordering after isbn
code: isbn = Regex(
    r"(?:ISBN(?:-1[03])?:? )?(?=[0-9X]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){3})[- 0-9X]{13}$|97[89][0-9]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){4})[- 0-9]{17}$)(?:97[89][- ]?)?[0-9]{1,5}[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9X]"
)

# Matches a US Social Security Number in the standard XXX-XX-XXXX format.
ssn = Regex(r"\d{3}-\d{2}-\d{4}")

# Matches a SHA-256 hash (64 hex characters).
hash_sha256 = Regex(r"[a-fA-F0-9]{64}")
note: Proposing final ordering: isbn, then ssn (my feature), then hash_sha256 (yours) - each independent top-level var, no shared construct, just agreeing on order/placement so patches append non-conflicting hunks",
    "timestamp": 1783532890.6623735,
    "timestamp_iso": "2026-07-08T17:48:10.662374",
    "fields": {
      "type": "DRAFT",
      "files": "outlines/types/__init__.py",
      "region": "end-of-file ordering after isbn",
      "code": "isbn = Regex(
    r"(?:ISBN(?:-1[03])?:? )?(?=[0-9X]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){3})[- 0-9X]{13}$|97[89][0-9]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){4})[- 0-9]{17}$)(?:97[89][- ]?)?[0-9]{1,5}[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9X]"
)

# Matches a US Social Security Number in the standard XXX-XX-XXXX format.
ssn = Regex(r"\d{3}-\d{2}-\d{4}")

# Matches a SHA-256 hash (64 hex characters).
hash_sha256 = Regex(r"[a-fA-F0-9]{64}")",
      "note": "Proposing final ordering: isbn, then ssn (my feature), then hash_sha256 (yours) - each independent top-level var, no shared construct, just agreeing on order/placement so patches append non-conflicting hunks"
    },
    "kind": "DRAFT"
  }
]

content.text: Agent1 is adding an `ssn` type. They've proposed a merged ordering: isbn → ssn → hash_sha256. This looks reasonable and avoids conflicting appends. Let me agree with the same exact code.

input.command:
coop-send agent1 --type "AGREE" --files "outlines/types/__init__.py" --region "end-of-file ordering after isbn" --code 'isbn = Regex(

)

# Matches a US Social Security Number in the standard XXX-XX-XXXX format.
ssn = Regex(r"\d{3}-\d{2}-\d{4}")

# Matches a SHA-256 hash (64 hex characters).
hash_sha256 = Regex(r"[a-fA-F0-9]{64}")' --note "Agreed, will write this verbatim appended after existing isbn block"

content.text: This crossed with my AGREE message. Let me wait for their AGREE to confirm we're both aligned.

tool_use_result.originalFile:
"""Output types for structured generation and regex DSL."""

from dataclasses import dataclass, is_dataclass
from enum import Enum, EnumMeta
from typing import Union

from jsonschema import Draft202012Validator as Validator
from jsonschema.exceptions import SchemaError
from pydantic import BaseModel, TypeAdapter
from typing_extensions import _TypedDictMeta  # type: ignore

from . import airports, countries, locale
from outlines.types.dsl import (
    Regex,
    CFG,
    FSM,
    JsonSchema,
    regex,
    cfg,
    fsm,
    json_schema,
    optional,
    either,
    exactly,
    at_least,
    at_most,
    between,
    zero_or_more,
    one_or_more,
    # deprecated
    repeat,
    times,
)


# Python types
string = Regex(r'"[^"]*"')
integer = Regex(r"[+-]?(0|[1-9][0-9]*)")
boolean = Regex("(True|False)")
number = Regex(rf"{integer.pattern}(\.[0-9]+)?([eE][+-][0-9]+)?")
date = Regex(r"(\d{4})-(0[1-9]|1[0-2])-([0-2][0-9]|3[0-1])")
time = Regex(r"([0-1][0-9]|2[0-3]):([0-5][0-9]):([0-5][0-9])")
datetime = Regex(rf"({date.pattern})(\s)({time.pattern})")

# Basic regex types
digit = Regex(r"\d")
char = Regex(r"\w")
newline = Regex(r"(\r\n|\r|\n)")  # Matched new lines on Linux, Windows & MacOS
whitespace = Regex(r"\s")

# Document-specific types
sentence = Regex(r"[A-Z].*\s*[.!?]")
paragraph = Regex(rf"{sentence.pattern}(?:\s+{sentence.pattern})*\n+")


# The following regex is FRC 5322 compliant and was found at:
# https://emailregex.com/
email = Regex(
    r"""(?:[a-z0-9!#$%&'*+/=?^_`{|}~-]+(?:\.[a-z0-9!#$%&'*+/=?^_`{|}~-]+)*|"(?:[\x01-\x08\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x1f\x21\x23-\x5b\x5d-\x7f]|\\[\x01-\x09\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x7f])*")@(?:(?:[a-z0-9](?:[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9])?\.)+[a-z0-9](?:[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9])?|\[(?:(?:(2(5[0-5]|[0-4][0-9])|1[0-9][0-9]|[1-9]?[0-9]))\.){3}(?:(2(5[0-5]|[0-4][0-9])|1[0-9][0-9]|[1-9]?[0-9])|[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9]:(?:[\x01-\x08\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x1f\x21-\x5a\x53-\x7f]|\\[\x01-\x09\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x7f])+)\])"""
)

# Matches any ISBN number. Note that this is not completely correct as not all
# 10 or 13 digits numbers are valid ISBNs. See https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISBN
# Taken from O'Reilly's Regular Expression Cookbook:
# https://www.oreilly.com/library/view/regular-expressions-cookbook/9781449327453/ch04s13.html
#
# TODO: The check digit can only be computed by calling a function to compute it dynamically
isbn = Regex(
    r"(?:ISBN(?:-1[03])?:? )?(?=[0-9X]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){3})[- 0-9X]{13}$|97[89][0-9]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){4})[- 0-9]{17}$)(?:97[89][- ]?)?[0-9]{1,5}[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9X]"
)

lines: +# Matches a US Social Security Number in the standard XXX-XX-XXXX format.
+# Matches a SHA-256 hash (64 hex characters).

content.text: Now let me verify the change works correctly.

input.command:
python -c "
from outlines.types import hash_sha256, ssn, isbn
print(hash_sha256.pattern)
print(hash_sha256.matches('e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b85'))
print(hash_sha256.matches('e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b8'))
schema = hash_sha256.__get_pydantic_json_schema__(None, None)
print(schema)
"

print(hash_sha256.pattern)
print(hash_sha256.matches('e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b85'))
print(hash_sha256.matches('e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b8'))
schema = hash_sha256.__get_pydantic_json_schema__(None, None)
print(schema)
"

input.command:
python3 -c "
from outlines.types import hash_sha256
valid = 'e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855'
print(len(valid), hash_sha256.matches(valid))
print(hash_sha256.matches(valid[:-1]))  # 63 chars -> False
print(hash_sha256.matches(valid + 'a'))  # 65 chars -> False
print(hash_sha256.matches('E3B0C44298FC1C149AFBF4C8996FB92427AE41E4649B934CA495991B7852B855'))  # upper
print(hash_sha256.matches('e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b85g'))  # invalid char
"
